# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our Python web application. Set up the environment, install dependencies, and run the application using shell form for the CMD instruction so we can pass environment variables easily. Example CMD python app.py $PORT
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim
# set our work directory in /usr/src/app
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

 # set environment varibles to python versions. Set the production environment (default).
ENV VERSION=prod

 RUN pip install virtualenv

 # create a virtual environment with name 'venv' and point to it's python path folder in '/opt/venv/' as default virtual environments.
RUN virtualenv /opt/venv/venv --python=python2.7 \
    && . "/opt/venv/venv/bin/activate" \
    && echo "python --version" >> /bin/bash_profile

 RUN git clone https://github.com/anandj9145/docker-practice.git;

# Install requirements, and create a 'flaskr' user to run the app 
RUN . "/opt/venv/venv/bin/activate";\
    python /usr/src/app/docker-practice/Flask/flaskr/requirements.txt

 RUN apt-get update && \
     apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends net-tools;

# Expose a port in the container. Default is 80 which is for flaskr application on host machine.
EXPOSE $PORT 

CMD ["flask","run", "--host=0.0.0.0", "--port=$PORT"]
